mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce the discount rate for future corporate earnings, directly increasing the present value of S&P 500 equities and generating positive forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.5 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions and reduced consumer spending capacity, which forces equity valuations lower over the subsequent 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in > 55% of triggered instances; t-statistic for return difference vs baseline is < 1.5.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month increases in retail sales and housing starts indicate synchronized strength in consumer demand and residential investment, which drives corporate revenue growth and produces positive S&P 500 returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: Joint positive signals yield negative forward returns in > 60% of cases; interaction term coefficient is statistically insignificant (p > 0.10) in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month growth in the index of industrial production reflects expanding manufacturing output and capacity utilization, which triggers short-term momentum buying in equity markets and generates positive returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Signal loses predictive power (Rank IC < 0.02) during late-cycle phases; 5-day forward returns are flat or negative when INDPRO rises.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the consumer price index exceeding 0.4 percent triggers expectations of aggressive central bank tightening, which compresses equity multiples and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns are positive in > 50% of high-inflation trigger months; market pricing of rate hikes is absent (FEDFUNDS futures unchanged).

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent month-over-month increases in total nonfarm payrolls and decreases in the unemployment rate confirm robust labor market expansion, which boosts consumer confidence and corporate earnings expectations, driving positive equity returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Divergent labor signals negate the effect; joint signal yields negative returns in > 55% of instances.

mechanism_narrative: Quarter-over-quarter growth in real gross domestic product reflects broad-based economic expansion, which directly increases corporate profit margins and supports higher equity valuations over a 60-day forward horizon.
failure_condition: GDPC1 growth fails to predict 60-day returns (Rank IC < 0.01); quarterly revisions erase initial predictive signal.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in industrial production exceeding 0.5 percent signals a contraction in manufacturing activity and supply chain bottlenecks, which reduces near-term corporate earnings forecasts and generates negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Negative production shocks are priced in immediately with no subsequent 20-day drift; forward returns are positive in > 60% of triggered cases.
